response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced full-stack engineer, but his profile does not match this role's core requirements. His C++ exposure is limited to a short stint at National Instruments (~7 months) and a thesis project, far from the mandatory 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. His database experience centers on MongoDB and MySQL rather than SQL Server/Oracle, and his financial-services exposure (RIDE Capital) was brief, TypeScript-based, and not in fixed income or capital markets. The gap between his strengths (web, Node.js, DLang, testing) and this role's demands is too wide to justify an interview.
strengths: Long, diverse engineering career with strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang async I/O at Sociomantic) showing performance-engineering aptitude
Some fintech exposure at RIDE Capital plus Java and SQL familiarity matching optional skills
concerns: No substantial or recent C++ experience, and none on Windows — the primary mandatory requirement
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain knowledge

key_factors.factor: No fixed income / capital markets domain expertise
Strong general engineering and performance background
